THU ’26
A Knight’s Brief · The Tribunal of the Storm
Sep 28 ~ Oct 03 · Tróia, Portugal · The Alchemy of a Storm

AI &
the Tribe

We can’t tame the storm — but we can decide how the Tribe meets it.

The industry walks into Tróia carrying the most negative numbers in its history, and the tools that caused them already in its hands. This brief stages the argument as a tribunal: four contenders, one verdict, and the place a Knight can stand to serve the Tribe.

The Charge ↓
Poster by Katsuya Terada
The charge before the bench

The contradiction on trial

THU’s instinct has always been to protect the maker — a generative-AI ban in its own Golden Ticket challenge, noai tags woven across its pages, a “tell the truth about your portfolio” ethos. Yet the Tribe arrives divided against itself: grieving, betrayed, and already using the very tools it fears.

That contradiction — not “is AI good or bad” — is the real matter on trial. The question is what posture lets THU stay THU while sending the Tribe home with more power than it walked in with.

52%
of developers call gen-AI bad for the industry — GDC 2026
36%
personally use it anyway · adoption ≠ approval
64%
visual artists negative — the most resistant discipline
The four contenders

The Tribunal

Each posture is summoned, steel-manned, and weighed — its case for, and the wound that undoes it.

Honored
CONTENDER · I

The Sanctuary

“Hold the line. THU is the last human stronghold.”

Lean fully into the protective posture — the LightBox Expo of Europe: an unapologetic celebration of human-driven creativity, an explicit no-AI showcase, a manifesto for the maker.

ForOn-brand & honest. The Tribe is grieving; give it a home that doesn’t flinch.
VsA museum. A sanctuary that won’t name the storm quietly insults the 36% already using AI to survive.
Rejected
CONTENDER · II

The Open Forge

“AI is a tool. Put the pipeline on the main stage.”

Program AI-assisted workflows, ethical tooling, and the Lionsgate/Runway reality as craft — meet adoption head-on and teach the room to wield it.

ForUseful. Honest about real adoption; practical for working pros.
VsThe Annecy misfire. 27 unions protested there. On THU’s stage it reads as selling out the people it protects.
Rejected
CONTENDER · III

The Silence

“Don’t make AI a theme at all. Avoid the landmine.”

Keep the storm off the program entirely. Protect the magic of the six days by refusing the most divisive subject in the industry.

ForNo controversy. No risk of becoming the headline.
VsAbdication. The one venue that could arm artists stays mute while the topic eats their careers. Silence reads as no answer.
The Verdict
CONTENDER · IV

The Alchemy — Agency, Not Anxiety

“Don’t debate the tool. Arm the Tribe.”

Reframe from “is AI good or bad” to “how do artists keep their power.” Center what artists can actually control: consent infrastructure, the three Cs, provenance as a human-made value mark, contract literacy, and the new legal premium on human authorship.

ForSoul kept, power gained. Honors abolitionist and pragmatist alike without forcing them to agree.
VsHarder to make. It needs real expertise, not a panel of feelings. (Solvable — see the Knight.)
The Judge’s ruling

The Alchemy Prevails

Adopt Contender IV — agency over anxiety — built on the emotional foundation of The Sanctuary. Keep the feeling: THU remains an unapologetic celebration of human creativity. Change the content: here is how you protect your work, price your humanity, read your contracts, and use or refuse these tools on your own terms.

THU should not relitigate “is AI art real” — that debate exhausts artists and changes no minds. Its rarest asset is trust, and the highest use of trust is converting fear into power. THU is one of the few venues credible enough to host the “third way” the industry is only beginning to find.

DECREE I

A track, not the whole event

Frame it as creative agency in the age of AI — kept in proportion to the six days, never the centre of gravity.

DECREE II

Name the three Cs in writing

Consent, credit, compensation — a short THU position that makes the noai posture articulated, not merely implied.

DECREE III

Teach leverage, not tools

Consent/legal literacy, provenance and human-made value, defensive tooling, honest studio-pipeline case studies — delivered straight.

DECREE IV

Avoid both failure modes

Not the silent museum, not the AI showcase. Protective and practical — what Annecy and SIGGRAPH could not manage.

Where the Knight stands

The Knight in the Storm

The same logic that chose the verdict points to the Knight’s seat. The agency path needs an operator’s mind, not a panel of feelings — and a translator the Tribe will actually trust. That is the opening, and it’s a rare one.

The Lane

Most AI voices in this room are hype merchants or ethics warriors. The open seat — and the right one for this Knight — is the calm operator who cuts the hype and shows how it’s really used. Someone who has shipped, can tell real from theater, and translates between the artists and the production reality. Not taking a side is the feature: the neutral everyone can talk to is the one who ends up holding the whole conversation.

“Be the one who can hold the whole conversation — not the loudest voice in it.”

The Move

i
Run the rooms. A short series of small, open, use-focused Night Talks — how it’s actually being used, what’s real vs. theater, a live workflow teardown. The Tribe teaches each other; the Knight connects the dots and hears everything.
ii
Hold the Oasis. A standing “find me to talk AI or pipeline” presence between sessions. Accessibility is how a go-to is made.
iii
Moderate the panel. On one of the last days, chair the spectrum of use — solo, indie, AAA, film. Ask the sharp questions without owning a side; close with the map. The chair who makes sense of it all is the authority.
iv
Own the thread. Past the week, propose an ongoing “AI in production” lane at THU — the durable version of the go-to seat, year over year.
The one guardrail —  This Tribe’s radar for self-promotion is sharp. Lead with service every time — useful, honest, genuinely curious about their work. The authority is a byproduct of being helpful, never the pitch. Earned that way, “the person you go to about AI at THU” is a seat that sticks.
For THU, in general

Standard-Setter, Not Fortress

Become the standard for human-centered creative practice — neither a fortress nor a tech demo. Three durable moves.

Declare the Values

Publish a clear, values-first AI position grounded in the three Cs and human authorship — so the noai posture is articulated, not just sensed.

Build Agency Year-Round

Run agency-building programming through Career Camp and the platform — leverage, literacy, protection — not one panel a year.

Protect the Footing

Use the Creative Resilience Fund framing to defend artists’ economic ground, not just their feelings — the soul André built, made practical.

Two cautions to carry into the storm

The law is mid-flight — the Thomson Reuters v. Ross appeal and the Sony music cases both land around now and could reframe the fair-use question before the Tribe gathers. And the single most important framing for this room: adoption is not approval — usage is up while sentiment sits at record lows.

Jason Cooper
THU Knight
Prepared for the Tribe · THU 2026 — The Alchemy of a Storm
“In the alchemy of a storm, believing is the fire that turns chaos into gold.”
